ucs.title: [LLM] TA578 contact-form / fake-DMCA lure → wscript JS dropper → msiexec Latrodectus install chain
ucs.description: Hunts the signature TA578 delivery sequence: a copyright/DMCA-themed email originating from a website contact form delivers a Google Firebase/Storage or Cloudflare R2 link to a ZIP containing a JScript (.js) file. User executes the .js via wscript.exe, which calls curl/msiexec to fetch and install a remote MSI (Latrodectus/IcedID/Bumblebee). This chain is uniquely characteristic of TA578 vs other loader brokers.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name=wscript.exe AND (Processes.process_name IN ("msiexec.exe","curl.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe") AND (Processes.process="*msiexec*/i*http*" OR Processes.process="*curl*-o*.msi*" OR Processes.process="*/i *https://*" OR Processes.process="*rundll32*nt_*")) by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer host [| tstats summariesonly=t values(All_Email.src_user) as sender values(All_Email.subject) as subject from datamodel=Email.All_Email where (All_Email.subject IN ("*DMCA*","*copyright*","*infringement*","*illegal use*","*image*","*photo*") OR All_Email.body="*contact form*") by All_Email.recipient | rename All_Email.recipient as host] | where isnotnull(sender) OR cmdline="*firebasestorage*" OR cmdline="*storage.googleapis*" OR cmdline="*r2.dev*" | `convert_timeformat`
ucs.kql: let suspectMail = EmailEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where Subject has_any ("DMCA","copyright","infringement","unauthorized use","my photo","illegal use of","stolen image") or SenderFromAddress has_any ("noreply@","no-reply@","contact@","webform@") | project MailTime=Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject; let scriptDrop = DeviceProcessEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "wscript.exe" | where FileName in~ ("msiexec.exe","curl.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe") | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("msiexec","/i http","/i https","firebasestorage.googleapis","storage.googleapis",".r2.dev","--ssl-no-revoke","-o C:\\Users",".msi") | project DeviceId, DeviceName, Timestamp, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessParentFileName, SHA256; let chainHost = scriptDrop | join kind=leftouter (DeviceFileEvents | where Timestamp > ago(7d) | where FileName endswith ".js" or FileName endswith ".jse" or FileName endswith ".zip" | where FolderPath has_any ("\\Downloads\\","\\Temp\\","\\AppData\\") | project DeviceId, JsFile=FileName, JsPath=FolderPath, JsTime=Timestamp) on DeviceId; chainHost | extend RecipientEmailAddress = tostring(AccountName) | join kind=leftouter suspectMail on RecipientEmailAddress | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, JsFile, JsPath, MailTime, SenderFromAddress, Subject | sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Proofpoint and Bitsight reporting on TA578 (the broker behind Latrodectus, IcedID, and Bumblebee delivery in 2023–2024) consistently shows the same kill chain: copyright/DMCA-themed lures delivered through legitimate website contact forms (T1566.003) → Firebase/Google Cloud Storage/Cloudflare R2 hosted ZIP → JScript (T1059.007) → msiexec /i pulling a remote MSI (T1218.007) → rundll32 running an ex
